key_points: An ecosystem consists of living organisms interacting with their physical environment
Biotic components include plants, animals and microorganisms
Abiotic components include air, water, soil, sunlight and temperature
Green plants are producers as they prepare food using photosynthesis
Consumers depend on other organisms for food
Decomposers break down dead organisms and recycle nutrients back to the soil
Food chains and food webs show feeding relationships in ecosystems
examples: Food chain example: Grass → Deer → Tiger
Plants are producers in most ecosystems
Herbivores such as cows and deer are primary consumers
Carnivores such as lions and tigers are secondary or tertiary consumers
Bacteria and fungi act as decomposers in ecosystems
formulas: Food chain → A sequence of organisms where each organism feeds on the previous one
Food web → A network of interconnected food chains
Trophic level → Each step in a food chain
Biological magnification → Increase in concentration of harmful chemicals along the food chain
exam_tips: Understand the structure of ecosystems and their components
Learn the roles of producers, consumers and decomposers
Practice drawing food chains and food webs
Remember the concept of trophic levels
Understand the concept of biological magnification
